title: The One With All The Resolutions

jokes.text: 1999, the year of Joey.
jokes.setup: New Year's Eve celebration, typical time for resolutions and optimistic declarations
jokes.punchline: Joey declares it 'the year of Joey' with complete sincerity, as if he's a brand or movement
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey narcissism delivered with perfect economy. Character-specific and quotable.

jokes.text: Can't kiss your sister. / Who's gonna kiss my sister? / Chandler.
jokes.setup: Ross establishing the obvious boundary about not kissing Monica
jokes.punchline: Joey immediately identifying Chandler as the sister-kisser, Chandler's horrified reaction
jokes.explanation: Well-structured revelation with good escalation. Chandler's dismay sells the moment.

jokes.text: Who would you rather have kiss your sister? / That's a good point.
jokes.setup: Ross's objection to Chandler kissing Monica
jokes.punchline: Joey's logic forcing Ross to accept there's no good alternative
jokes.explanation: Joey's unexpected wisdom moment. Character-appropriate problem-solving.

jokes.text: Ross wants to kiss you at midnight. / So obvious. Why doesn't he just ask?
jokes.setup: Joey delivering Ross's request indirectly
jokes.punchline: Phoebe calling out the obviousness of the indirect approach
jokes.explanation: Phoebe's directness cuts through the social awkwardness perfectly.

jokes.text: So that do anything for you?
jokes.setup: Joey and Monica just kissed at midnight
jokes.punchline: Joey immediately asking for sexual feedback like it's a product review
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey line - treating romance like a casual transaction with zero self-awareness.

jokes.text: Just the one divorce in '99. [BLOWS NOISE MAKER]
jokes.setup: Ross trying to be optimistic about no divorces, reminded his isn't final
jokes.punchline: Immediately revising to 'just one' while maintaining celebratory tone
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about Ross's serial divorcing with perfect casual delivery. The noise maker adds absurdity.

jokes.text: Do you want us to leave the room or?
jokes.setup: Phoebe declaring she's going to make herself happy
jokes.punchline: Chandler's immediate assumption it's sexual
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler misdirection. Predictable but well-executed.

jokes.text: Mine is to pilot a commercial jet.
jokes.setup: Discussion of achievable New Year's resolutions
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's completely unrealistic and dangerous ambition stated matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Pure Phoebe absurdism. The casual delivery of something impossible and terrifying.

jokes.text: Now you only have to find a planeload of people whose resolution is to plummet to their deaths.
jokes.setup: Phoebe's unrealistic pilot ambition
jokes.punchline: Chandler pointing out the logical deadly outcome with elaborate phrasing
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark escalation with sophisticated wordplay. 'Plummet to their deaths' is perfectly Chandler.

jokes.text: She has a better chance of sprouting wings and flying up your nose than you do of not making fun of us.
jokes.setup: Phoebe suggesting Chandler's resolution should be not making fun of friends
jokes.punchline: Joey's bizarrely specific and impossible imagery to describe Chandler's inability to stop
jokes.explanation: Unexpectedly creative imagery from Joey. The visual absurdity elevates a simple comparison.

jokes.text: Paying me the 50 bucks can be the 'new thing you do' that day.
jokes.setup: Chandler accepting the bet about not making fun of friends
jokes.punchline: Immediately connecting his inevitable victory to Ross's resolution
jokes.explanation: Smart callback that shows Chandler's confidence while tying plot threads together.

jokes.text: I would love it if one of those was true.
jokes.setup: Ross explaining why he wants to learn guitar - his resume lists special skills
jokes.punchline: Admitting none of his listed skills are real
jokes.explanation: Relatable workplace dishonesty delivered with Ross's earnest embarrassment.

jokes.text: Really? Who have you taught? / Well, I taught me and I loved me.
jokes.setup: Ross asking about Phoebe's teaching credentials
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's self-referential logic that makes no sense but sounds profound
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe logic - circular reasoning that somehow sounds wise and silly simultaneously.

jokes.text: Who, from what I hear, needs to keep his stapler in his desk drawer, if you know what I'm talking about.
jokes.setup: Rachel gossiping about Blair's boyfriend
jokes.punchline: Euphemistic reference to erectile dysfunction via office supply metaphor
jokes.explanation: Rachel's attempt at sophisticated innuendo. Slightly clunky but character-appropriate.

jokes.text: You know, kind of like a public service. Doesn't mean I'm a gossip. I mean, would you call Ted Koppel a gossip?
jokes.setup: Rachel defending her gossip habit
jokes.punchline: Comparing celebrity gossip to serious journalism via Ted Koppel
jokes.explanation: Rachel's delusional self-justification with unexpected highbrow reference.

jokes.text: Well, if Ted Koppel talked about his coworkers' botched boob jobs... yeah, I would.
jokes.setup: Rachel's Ted Koppel comparison
jokes.punchline: Chandler imagining serious news anchor discussing plastic surgery
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation that deflates Rachel's pretension perfectly.

jokes.text: Well, they were like this. [demonstrates with hands]
jokes.setup: Discussion of botched boob jobs
jokes.punchline: Rachel physically demonstrating the surgical failure
jokes.explanation: Simple visual gag that relies on basic body humor. Functional but not sophisticated.

jokes.text: Elizabeth Hornswoggle. / Hornswoggle.
jokes.setup: Ross proudly announcing his date's name
jokes.punchline: The absurd surname 'Hornswoggle' and Chandler's incredulous repetition
jokes.explanation: Silly name humor. The word itself is funny but setup is basic.

jokes.text: No, no, I just think that maybe I've heard it somewhere before. / Oh, really? Where? / Somewhere funny I'll bet.
jokes.setup: Chandler trying not to mock the ridiculous name due to his bet
jokes.punchline: His obvious struggle to contain sarcasm about where he might have heard 'Hornswoggle'
jokes.explanation: Good character conflict - Chandler's sarcasm fighting his resolution. Shows internal struggle well.

jokes.text: Hornswoggle? / Oh, this must be killing you.
jokes.setup: Phoebe hearing the ridiculous name
jokes.punchline: Immediately recognizing how tortured Chandler must be not being able to mock it
jokes.explanation: Phoebe showing awareness of group dynamics. Meta-humor about Chandler's character trait.

jokes.text: I don't know the actual names of the chords, but I made up names for the way my hand looks while I'm doing them.
jokes.setup: Phoebe offering to teach guitar properly
jokes.punchline: Revealing her completely unorthodox and visual-based method
jokes.explanation: Perfectly Phoebe - rejecting conventional knowledge for personal intuitive system.

jokes.text: So then, this is 'Bear Claw.' Okay, 'Turkey Leg'... and 'Old Lady.'
jokes.setup: Phoebe's visual chord naming system
jokes.punchline: The specific ridiculous names she's invented
jokes.explanation: Memorable absurd names that become recurring elements. Creative and quotable.

jokes.text: You know, some might find it amusing. I myself find it regular.
jokes.setup: Ross witnessing Phoebe's unconventional teaching method
jokes.punchline: His awkward attempt to be diplomatically critical using 'regular' as an insult
jokes.explanation: Ross's intellectual snobbery expressed through faint praise. 'Regular' as dismissal is perfectly Ross.

jokes.text: All right, let's just say I found out something about someone... and let's just say... she's gonna keep it.
jokes.setup: Rachel claiming she didn't gossip all day
jokes.punchline: Immediately demonstrating gossip behavior while claiming to resist it
jokes.explanation: Good irony showing Rachel can't help herself even while trying to reform.

jokes.text: Ooh. You nailed the Old Lady. Oh! Oh, ha, ha!
jokes.setup: Phoebe congratulating Ross on mastering a chord position
jokes.punchline: The unintended sexual innuendo of 'nailed the Old Lady' and the awkward realization
jokes.explanation: Accidental double entendre. Relies on sexual misunderstanding but the setup was established.

jokes.text: Give me your hands. Strings. Pick.
jokes.setup: Joey admitting he touched guitars at the store despite Phoebe's prohibition
jokes.punchline: Phoebe discovering evidence of his transgression like a detective
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Phoebe's teaching tyranny. Physical evidence humor works well.

jokes.text: Do you want to learn to play guitar? / Yes. / Then don't touch one!
jokes.setup: Standard guitar learning expectations
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's completely counterintuitive teaching philosophy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic. The perfect contradiction delivered with Phoebe's authority.

jokes.text: Ross is wearing leather pants. Does nobody else see that Ross is wearing leather pants? Someone comment on the pants.
jokes.setup: Ross enters wearing leather pants, everyone politely ignoring the obvious
jokes.punchline: Monica's desperate attempt to get someone else to address the elephant in the room
jokes.explanation: Good setup for leather pants subplot. Monica's directness contrasts with group politeness.

jokes.text: See, people like Ross don't generally wear these types of pants. You see, they're very tight. Maybe there's something in that area?
jokes.setup: Monica trying to get people to notice Ross's inappropriate pants
jokes.punchline: Her increasingly desperate and inappropriate suggestions about what might be enhanced
jokes.explanation: Monica's social awkwardness escalating. Setup for bigger pants comedy to come.

jokes.text: I've never owned a really good-smelling pair of pants before.
jokes.setup: Ross explaining his leather pants purchase decision
jokes.punchline: Prioritizing scent over all other clothing considerations
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ross logic - overthinking leading to bizarre priorities. Memorably absurd reasoning.

jokes.text: You look like a freak. Yeah, awful. / Awful.
jokes.setup: Ross asking for honest opinions about his leather pants
jokes.punchline: Joey and Chandler's brutal honesty delivered in unison
jokes.explanation: Straightforward honesty humor. The synchronized delivery adds impact.

jokes.text: It's my New Year's resolution. / To blind my child?
jokes.setup: Monica explaining why she's taking lots of photos
jokes.punchline: Ross's assumption that flash photography is child abuse
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with Ross's parental paranoia. Efficient dialogue.

jokes.text: My God, these pants. I'm burning up.
jokes.setup: Ross on his date wearing the leather pants
jokes.punchline: The pants are causing him physical torment
jokes.explanation: Setup for the pants disaster. Ross's suffering for fashion choices.

jokes.text: Oh, God. She wants to snuggle now? What, is she trying to kill me? It's like a volcano in here.
jokes.setup: Ross already overheating in leather pants
jokes.punchline: Physical intimacy becoming a life-threatening situation with volcanic imagery
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Ross's physical comedy predicament with dramatic metaphor.

jokes.text: [FARTING NOISE] / That was just the pants and the couch.
jokes.setup: Leather pants making noise against couch
jokes.punchline: Ross's mortified explanation of the sound
jokes.explanation: Basic embarrassment humor. Leather-on-couch noise is predictable but effective.

jokes.text: G-sharp? / G-sharp? Have you been studying the real names of the chords?
jokes.setup: Joey accidentally using proper musical terminology
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's outrage at his betrayal of her method
jokes.explanation: Good character conflict escalation. Phoebe's possessiveness over her teaching method.

jokes.text: No, but you're questioning my method. / No, I'm not questioning it, Phoebe. I'm saying it's stupid.
jokes.setup: Phoebe accusing Joey of questioning her teaching
jokes.punchline: Joey's blunt clarification that he's not questioning, he's condemning
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with Joey's characteristic bluntness making things worse.

jokes.text: Your other student was you!
jokes.setup: Phoebe claiming other students didn't think she was stupid
jokes.punchline: Joey pointing out the logical flaw in her self-taught credentials
jokes.explanation: Smart callback that deflates Phoebe's argument with simple logic.

jokes.text: And look. Ooh, there's a picture of him with a kid. Yeah, and the kid's got a guitar!
jokes.setup: Joey finding a real guitar teacher in the phone book
jokes.punchline: His excitement over proof that students are actually allowed to hold instruments
jokes.explanation: Good contrast humor highlighting how absurd Phoebe's method is.

jokes.text: But don't come crying to me when everyone is sick and tired of hearing you play 'Bad, Bad Leroy Brown.'
jokes.setup: Phoebe's bitter response to Joey choosing a real teacher
jokes.punchline: Her prediction that proper teaching will only lead to boring, mainstream music
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe - dismissing conventional success as artistic failure. Specific song choice adds authenticity.

jokes.text: Laundry. Huh. Is that my new nickname? / You know what your nickname is, Mr. Big...
jokes.setup: Monica using 'laundry' as code for their secret relationship
jokes.punchline: Sexual innuendo interrupted by disaster, leaving audience to imagine
jokes.explanation: Setup for physical disaster. The interrupted innuendo builds anticipation.

jokes.text: No, I got really hot in my leather pants so I took them off. But they must have shrunk from the sweat or something. Or my legs expanded from the heat.
jokes.setup: Ross calling Joey for help from bathroom
jokes.punchline: His scientific theories for why the pants won't go back on
jokes.explanation: Ross's intellectual approach to physical comedy. Multiple theories show his overthinking.

jokes.text: Oh. That is quite a situation.
jokes.setup: Ross explaining his pants predicament
jokes.punchline: Joey's massive understatement of the crisis
jokes.explanation: Joey's casual response to Ross's panic. Good understatement for comic contrast.

jokes.text: And the lotion and the powder have made a paste.
jokes.setup: Ross following Joey's advice to use lotion and powder
jokes.punchline: The combination creating an even worse situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation. The word 'paste' is viscerally funny and creates vivid mental image.

jokes.text: Well, if the paste matches the pants, you can make yourself paste pants and she won't know the difference.
jokes.setup: Ross's paste problem
jokes.punchline: Joey's completely insane solution involving making pants out of body paste
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd Joey logic. 'Paste pants' is memorably ridiculous and shows his problem-solving style.

jokes.text: I don't care, Rach. Look, I'm tired of being the guy who knows all the secrets and can't tell anyone.
jokes.setup: Rachel trying to tell Joey a secret
jokes.punchline: Joey's existential crisis about being the secret repository
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor about Joey's role in the group dynamic. Shows character growth and self-awareness.

jokes.text: Hey, can you turn the lights off? / No. Let's just leave the lights on.
jokes.setup: Ross preparing to emerge from bathroom in compromised state
jokes.punchline: Elizabeth's firm rejection suggesting she wants to see the disaster
jokes.explanation: Building tension for the reveal. Elizabeth's insistence adds to Ross's mortification.

jokes.text: Oh, my God. / I had a problem.
jokes.setup: Ross emerging from bathroom
jokes.punchline: Elizabeth's horrified reaction and Ross's massive understatement
jokes.explanation: Payoff for the building tension. Visual comedy we can only imagine enhanced by understated dialogue.

jokes.text: I can't talk to you. I don't have a fancy ad in the yellow pages.
jokes.setup: Joey trying to reconcile with Phoebe
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's bitter reference to his preference for 'real' teachers
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Phoebe's hurt feelings through sarcasm about credentials.

jokes.text: [Guitar breaks] / Was the chord at least right before...? / No.
jokes.setup: Joey finally getting to touch a guitar after reconciling
jokes.punchline: Immediately breaking it, then learning his technique was also wrong
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing and escalation. The additional 'no' adds insult to injury efficiently.

jokes.text: Ben drew a picture of you. You're a cowboy. / Because of the leather pants?
jokes.setup: Monica trying to cheer up Ross
jokes.punchline: Ross realizing his fashion disaster inspired his son's art
jokes.explanation: Sweet irony that Ross's embarrassment became his child's positive image of him.

jokes.text: Ooh, you know, they had some... with fringe all down the sides.
jokes.setup: Ross considering getting another pair of leather pants
jokes.punchline: Rachel's suggestion of an even more ridiculous version
jokes.explanation: Escalation of the absurd fashion choices. Fringe adds visual comedy element.

jokes.text: I would make a good cowboy.
jokes.setup: Ross processing the cowboy comparison
jokes.punchline: His completely sincere belief in his cowboy potential
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ross delusion. His earnestness about being a cowboy despite the disaster.

jokes.text: I'm gonna go to the airport. If I hang around there long enough... someone's bound to leave one of those planes unattended.
jokes.setup: Everyone leaving for their evening activities
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's casual plan to steal a commercial aircraft
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of her pilot dream into casual felony. The matter-of-fact delivery is perfect.

jokes.text: Hey, Joe, would you mind going over into Chandler's bedroom... and getting that book back that he borrowed from me?
jokes.setup: Rachel asking Joey to do a simple favor
jokes.punchline: Obviously setting him up to discover Monica and Chandler together
jokes.explanation: Setup for the big reveal. Transparent but necessary for plot advancement.

jokes.text: What's the thing you know? / You tell me what you know. / I can't tell you. / Then I can't.
jokes.setup: Rachel and Joey both knowing about Monica and Chandler but unable to admit it
jokes.punchline: The circular logic trap of mutual secret-keeping
jokes.explanation: Good tension building through circular conversation. Shows the absurdity of secret-keeping.

jokes.text: Chandler and Monica? Oh, this is unbelievable. / How long have you known? / Too long. Oh, my God.
jokes.setup: The secret finally coming out
jokes.punchline: Joey's 'too long' suggesting he's been suffering with this knowledge
jokes.explanation: Payoff for Joey's earlier complaint about bearing secrets. Relief humor.

jokes.text: Oh, I am going to go for a walk in the rain. Ha, ha.
jokes.setup: Phoebe using rain as excuse not to fly
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicting herself with enthusiasm about rain activities
jokes.explanation: Phoebe's quick pivot showing her adaptability and positive attitude.

jokes.text: That's weird. I bet they're doing it.
jokes.setup: Rachel and Joey both suddenly wanting to go out in the rain
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's immediate assumption of sexual motivation
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe directness. Her assumption is wrong but in character.

jokes.text: Pheebs? Flying a jet? Better make it a spaceship so you can get back to your home planet.
jokes.setup: Chandler finally breaking and mocking everyone
jokes.punchline: Calling Phoebe an alien with elaborate spaceship imagery
jokes.explanation: Great Chandler escalation combining her flying dream with alien jokes. Worth losing money.

jokes.text: And Ross. Phone call for you today. Tom Jones. He wants his pants back.
jokes.setup: Chandler continuing his mockery spree
jokes.punchline: Connecting Ross's leather pants to Tom Jones's signature style
jokes.explanation: Clever pop culture reference that perfectly captures Ross's fashion mistake.

jokes.text: And Hornswoggle? What, are you dating a character from Fraggle Rock?
jokes.setup: Chandler's final mockery target
jokes.punchline: Comparing the ridiculous name to children's television characters
jokes.explanation: Good pop culture reference that emphasizes the name's absurdity. Fraggle Rock fits perfectly.
